channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why it’s so challenging to deploy AI on space missions
item.description: Everything is more difficult in space, and learning software is no exception. But the pressure to push AI into everything can’t be denied. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Optus once again crowned the fastest mobile network in latest network report
item.description: Opensignal has released its latest report on Australian phone network experiences, and Optus has won the most awards once again.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Xiaomi 15, Honor Magic 7, and more Android phones confirmed to feature the Snapdragon 8 Elite
item.description: Several Android manufacturers have confirmed that their upcoming devices will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: Facebook owner Meta is bringing back facial recognition tech. Here’s why
item.description: Three years after Meta shut down facial recognition software on Facebook amid a groundswell of privacy and regulator pushback, the social media giant said on Tuesday it is testing the service again as part of a crackdown on “celeb bait” scams.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Jet-tracker who watched Zuckerberg, Trump, Musk says Meta has banned his accounts
item.description: Meta has removed accounts that track the plane movements of its CEO Mark Zuckerberg, X owner Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Kim Kardashian, former President Trump and others, account owner Jack Sweeney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Will LLMs become the ultimate mediators for better and for worse? DeepMind researchers and Reddit users seem to agree on that
item.description: Researchers at Google DeepMind have explored the use of AI to mediate political disputes - and the results were surprising.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: We need a national STEM Act to stem the risk of a declining America
item.description: Wars have unintended consequences, and positive results can emerge from tragedy. For proof, just look at the list from NATO of some unexpected outcomes of R&D that led to society-changing benefits.
item.dc:creator: Frederic Bertley, Fast Company

item.title: Flying air taxis move closer to US takeoff with issuing of FAA rule
item.description: Federal regulators are giving a strong push to electric-powered air taxis by issuing a final rule for operating the aircraft and how pilots will be trained to fly them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: You’re going to have a robot coworker sooner than you think
item.description: They marched in line, interacted with guests, and poured drinks while making small talk. The vision of the future Elon Musk put forward with his Optimus humanoid robots at Tesla’s recent Cybercab event earlier this month was as astounding as it was unbelievable.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CIOs recalibrate multicloud strategies as challenges remain
item.description: Cloud strategies are undergoing a sea change of late, with CIOs becoming more intentional about making the most of multiple clouds.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don’t panic. Here’s how to find your photos after iOS 18 update
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on the iOS 18 Photos app: Revamped layout, customizable collections and easier photo organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Perplexity is sued by Dow Jones and ‘New York Post’ for copyright infringement
item.description: Rupert Murdoch’s Dow Jones, which is the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, and the New York Post both sued AI search startup Perplexity on Monday for infringing copyrighted content. The News Corp.-owned companies accused Perplexity of a “massive amount of illegal copying” of their work.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: NYC Department of Education builds the pipeline for future cybersecurity professionals
item.description: There’s been a lot of talk over the past few years about a shortage of skilled cybersecurity talent, but from where we sit at the NYC DOE Division of Information and Instructional Technology (DIIT), we see budding security pros all around us.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rust 1.82 brings Cargo info subcommand
item.description: Rust 1.82, the newest version of the popular memory-safe programming language, fulfills a longstanding request for displaying information in the Cargo package manager registry. Also with Rust 1.82, Apple macOS on 64-bit Arm now offers Rust’s highest guarantee of functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Effectiveness of cybersecurity penetration testing depends on what you penetrate
item.description: "We get to do all kinds of really interesting, often nefarious things. Our job is to think like the bad guys do, and to try and break in," said Rob Olson. The post Effectiveness of cybersecurity penetration testing depends on what you penetrate first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap
item.description: Addressing the federal cybersecurity skills gap requires a multifaceted approach in coordination with the private sector. The post Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM works to address the developer skills gap with AI
item.description: When IBM announced in May 2023 that its generative artificial intelligence tools were in use by its AI assistant, watsonx, to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications by translating COBOL code into Java, it was just the beginning of its efforts to leverage generative AI for developers
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Big Tech won big against regulation in California this year
item.description: Victories attributed to aggressive lobbying and political donations, Silicon Valley's massive contributions to state tax revenue, legislators' technology-stock holdings, and anti-regulatory ideology.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: NoviOcean's Revolutionary Hybrid Energy Platform: A Game-Changer in Renewable Energy
item.description: NoviOcean's innovative hybrid energy platform combines wind, solar, and wave power to provide consistent clean energy around the clock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 11 open source AI projects that developers will love
item.description: The most creative and impactful ideas in software often appear as open source. Sharing code invites a global community of contributors to work together to drive a project forward. Artificial intelligence is no exception.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stopping the rot in AI spending
item.description: They say that half the money spent on advertising is wasted, but the problem is figuring out which half. If that’s true, the problem is arguably worse for AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EVs become flashpoint in pivotal Michigan Senate race
item.description: Electric vehicles (EVs) are emerging as a flashpoint in Michigan’s pivotal Senate race. Republican former Rep. Mike Rogers (Mich.) has put Democratic Rep. Elissa Slotkin (Mich.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: This 10-second tweak will bring new life to any browser
item.description: When it comes to most home screens—on your phone, your computer, you name it—you’ve got endless-seeming options for customizing and taking control. The one canvas you can’t easily paint, curiously enough, is your browser’s New Tab page.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data center provider fakes Tier 4 data center certificate to bag $11M SEC deal
item.description: Deepak Jain, CEO of a Maryland-based IT services firm, has been indicted for fraud and making false statements after allegedly falsifying a Tier 4 data center certification to secure a $10.7 million contract with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple Pay made wallets digital, and it’s only the beginning
item.description: Apple turned your iPhone into your wallet one decade ago. Ten years on, and the company continues to extend the digitization of personal payments, with small-scale loans, car rentals, and everything else that used to occupy space in your pocket.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Global cybersecurity talent gap widens
item.description: Despite some recent positive news for tech hiring, a growing cybersecurity talent shortage threatens to hamstring businesses. Data from CyberSeek shows that in the U.S., some 265,000 more cybersecurity workers are needed to solve current staffing needs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4
